calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's motion to compel further responses to the requests for admissions is DENIED.

First, Plaintiff did not meet and confer in good faith. The parties agreed to defer discovery until the court ruled on the motion for judgment on the pleadings. (Fong Decl., pars. 10-11, Ex. C.) Even though the motion for judgment on the pleadings dismissed most of plaintiff's case and rendered much of the discovery irrelevant, plaintiff failed to meet and confer again after the court's ruling.

Second, Plaintiff failed to file a separate statement in compliance with CRC 3.1345(a)(1) [separate statement required to compel further responses to requests for admission].

Third, Plaintiff failed to demonstrate the RFA requests for which he seeks to compel further responses are appropriate in this case, which has been narrowed to the CPRA action. "When a party seeks pretrial discovery in a proceeding to enforce the California Public Records Act 'the trial court must determine whether the discovery sought is necessary to resolve whether the agency has a duty to disclose, and additionally consider whether the request is justified given the need for an expeditious resolution.'" (County of San Benito v. Superior Court (2023) 96 Cal.App.5th 243, 249.) Discovery in an action under the California Public Records Act is "circumscribed by its relevance to the 'narrow issue: whether a public agency has an obligation to disclose the records that the petitioner has requested'" (County of San Benito, 96 Cal.App.5th at 254.) Plaintiff failed to show the discovery sought is necessary to resolve whether Defendant has a duty to disclose the records he has requested.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to the special interrogatories is DENIED.

First, plaintiff did not meet and confer in good faith. The parties agreed to defer discovery until the court ruled on the motion for judgment on the pleadings. (Fong Decl., pars. 10-11, Ex. C.) Even though the motion for judgment on the pleadings dismissed most of plaintiff's case and rendered much of the discovery irrelevant, plaintiff failed to meet and confer again after the court's ruling. "When a party seeks pretrial discovery in a proceeding to enforce the California Public Records Act'the trial court must determine whether the discovery sought is necessary to resolve whether the agency has a duty to disclose, and additionally consider whether the request is justified given the need for an expeditious resolution.'" (County of San Benito v. Superior Court (2023) 96 Cal.App.5th 243, 249.) Discovery in an action under the California Public Records Act is "circumscribed by its relevance to the 'narrow issue: whether a public agency has an obligation to disclose the records that the petitioner has requested'" (County of San Benito, 96 Cal.App.5th at 254.) Second, plaintiff failed to file a separate statement in compliance with CRC 3.1345(a)(2) [separate statement required to compel further responses to interrogatories].

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to the requests for production is DENIED. First, plaintiff did not meet and confer in good faith. The parties agreed to defer discovery until the court ruled on the motion for judgment on the pleadings. (Fong Decl., pars. 10-11, Ex. C.) Even though the motion for judgment on the pleadings dismissed most of plaintiff's case and rendered much of the discovery irrelevant, plaintiff failed to meet and confer again after the court's ruling. "When a party seeks pretrial discovery in a proceeding to enforce the California Public Records Act'the trial court must determine whether the discovery sought is necessary to resolve whether the agency has a duty to disclose, and additionally consider whether the request is justified given the need for an expeditious resolution.'" (County of San Benito v. Superior Court (2023) 96 Cal.App.5th 243, 249.) Discovery in an action under the California Public Records Act is "circumscribed by its relevance to the 'narrow issue: whether a public agency has an obligation to disclose the records that the petitioner has requested'" (County of San Benito, 96 Cal.App.5th at 254.) Second, plaintiff failed to file a separate statement in compliance with CRC 3.1345(a)(5) [separate statement required to compel further production of documents].

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard C. Darwin, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RCD)

Motion To Compel Entry Of Default And For Order Directing The Clerk To Enter Default Under Ccp 585
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's Motion To Compel Entry Of Default And For Order Directing The Clerk To Enter Default Under Code of Civil Procedure Section 585 is DENIED.

The operative complaint before the court is the First Amended Complaint, filed September 25, 2025. Defendant filed an answer on November 26, 2025. No basis for entry of default currently exists. The motion, thus, is denied.

Order sign.
